title: Testing in Rust
content:
### Testing in Rust

Rust has built-in testing support with the `#[test]` attribute and `cargo test` command.

---

### Test Types

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Unit tests | Test individual functions, placed in same file |
| Integration tests | Test public API from external perspective |
| Doc tests | Run code examples in documentation |

---

### Running Tests

```bash
# Run all tests
cargo test

# Run tests with filter
cargo test test_name

# Run ignored tests
cargo test -- --ignored

# Show output
cargo test -- --nocapture

# Run specific test file
cargo test --test integration_test
```

### Test Organization

```rust
// In src/lib.rs or src/file.rs
pub fn add(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32 {
    a + b
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    #[test]
    fn test_add() {
        assert_eq!(add(2, 3), 5);
    }
}
```

> **Key Point:** Unit tests go in a `#[cfg(test)]` module in the same file as the code. Integration tests go in the `tests/` directory. Use `#[should_panic]` for tests that expect panics. The `#[cfg(test)]` attribute ensures test code is not compiled in release builds.

quiz.question: Which command runs all tests in a Rust project?
